acceptance_targets: [CH13-F001] PASS if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:336 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:344 contains (a) a grammatically complete risk sentence with explicit causality (including "due to" or equivalent), and (b) at least one concrete, physical consequence tied to a specific person in-scene; FAIL if stakes remain only abstract policy nouns.

[CH13-F002] PASS if revised span snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:3 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:53 include at least three distinct paragraphs with explicit Lena interiority (thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment) and no run longer than six consecutive paragraphs without such access; FAIL otherwise.

[CH13-F003] PASS if the cited span contains no more than two uses of "looked/looked at" after revision and each replacement conveys a distinct perceptual mode (movement, touch, inference, or avoidance); FAIL if the repeated template still dominates.

[CH13-F004] PASS if revised span snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:223 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:285 includes at least one clearly distinguishable syntax/rhythm marker per speaker (Lena, Eleanor, Kline) plus at least two pressure artifacts (interruption, cut-off, broken clause, or overlap); FAIL if lines remain interchangeable in register.

[CH13-F005] PASS if, by the end of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:111 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:123, Thomas performs a decision/action that is explicitly reflected in at least one assignment or restriction in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:305 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:310; FAIL if he remains only a worried observer.

[CH13-F006] PASS if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:55 is rewritten to depict behavior without profile-taxonomy terms (e.g., "stress tells", "register", "signature") while still making Lena's pressure shift legible; FAIL if metadata-like wording remains.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:336 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:344 contains (a) a grammatically complete risk sentence with explicit causality (including "due to" or equivalent), and (b) at least one concrete, physical consequence tied to a specific person in-scene; FAIL if stakes remain only abstract policy nouns.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift at the chapter's pressure point: the final risk statement retreats into bureaucratic euphemism ("Foreseeable blind-spot vulnerability") and clipped grammar (missing function word in "due legal"), defusing bodily consequence right where dread should peak.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the close at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:336, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:338, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:344 so the memo line remains legally precise but is grammatically complete and immediately paired with concrete human consequence (named person/path at risk if the lane fails). Keep the procedural frame, but do not let abstraction be the only texture.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised span snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:3 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:53 include at least three distinct paragraphs with explicit Lena interiority (thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment) and no run longer than six consecutive paragraphs without such access; FAIL otherwise.
findings.problem: Opening stretch reads like screenplay blocking: the chapter runs more than ten consecutive paragraphs before clear interior access to Lena (first explicit interior beat appears at line 55), weakening close-third anchoring.
findings.rewrite_direction: Inject focalized interior beats into the opening run at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:21, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:35, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:49 so pressure registers as thought/sensation/memory before the line-55 pivot.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the cited span contains no more than two uses of "looked/looked at" after revision and each replacement conveys a distinct perceptual mode (movement, touch, inference, or avoidance); FAIL if the repeated template still dominates.
findings.problem: Recurring "X looked..." construction appears well beyond twice, creating a visible prose tic that flattens moment-to-moment perception.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:83, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:151, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:225, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:297, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:326, replace at least four "looked" clauses with differentiated physical or cognitive actions tied to each character's intent.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised span snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:223 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:285 includes at least one clearly distinguishable syntax/rhythm marker per speaker (Lena, Eleanor, Kline) plus at least two pressure artifacts (interruption, cut-off, broken clause, or overlap); FAIL if lines remain interchangeable in register.
findings.problem: During the corridor standoff, Lena, Eleanor, and Kline converge into similarly polished policy syntax. Under scene pressure, idiolect separation softens and the exchange loses interruption/friction texture.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the confrontation at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:223 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:285 so Lena's stress pattern shifts into harder compact bursts, Kline uses more field-ops idiom and contractor pragmatics, and Eleanor's legalistic control shows through revisions/qualifiers rather than matching everyone else's cadence.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, by the end of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:111 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:123, Thomas performs a decision/action that is explicitly reflected in at least one assignment or restriction in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:305 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:310; FAIL if he remains only a worried observer.
findings.problem: Thomas is framed as the imminent target but remains mostly reactive; he exits without making a consequential decision that changes the tactical reality, reducing character-driven causality.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:69 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:123, give Thomas one explicit decision with operational consequence (for example, refusing/signed condition on the limits sheet, route change, or access concession) that materially affects the later deployment plan at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:305 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:310.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:55 is rewritten to depict behavior without profile-taxonomy terms (e.g., "stress tells", "register", "signature") while still making Lena's pressure shift legible; FAIL if metadata-like wording remains.
findings.problem: Mechanical token reuse: "stress tells" reads like imported style-bible taxonomy instead of lived narration, briefly exposing the calibration scaffolding inside the prose.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the label-driven phrasing at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:55 with embodied behavior (breath, cadence shift, verbal compression) so Lena's stress transition is shown rather than categorized.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
